basics.name: Runjia Zhang
basics.headline: Graduate Software Engineer @ EROAD | Product Owner @ AUSS | Penultimate-Year SWE Student

summary.title: Summary
summary.content: Penultimate-year Software Engineering student at the University of Auckland, currently working part-time as a Graduate Software Engineer at EROAD and serving as Product Owner on a full-stack platform for AUSS — the largest sports club at UoA. Hands-on experience building AI agents with Microsoft Copilot Studio and custom MCP (Model Context Protocol) integrations, designing cloud-native pipelines on AWS (S3, Lambda) with Kubernetes-ready deployments, and automating production-critical workflows with Power Automate and Snowflake. Comfortable across the stack — React/TypeScript, Node.js/Express, Prisma + PostgreSQL, real-time telemetry (MQTT + Socket.io), and embedded firmware (ESP32). Strong in Agile delivery, Requirements Engineering, and turning ambiguous business problems into reliable, shipped software.

items.description:
• Fuel Data Translation Pipeline (AI + MCP): Designed and built an AI-driven data translation pipeline integrating Microsoft Copilot Studio with EROAD's fuel transaction API via two custom Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers. The AI generates, deploys, and validates a JavaScript translation script once; the converter then runs deterministically with no further AI calls — making it fast, cheap, and auditable. Architected for AWS deployment using S3 for artifact storage and Lambda for serverless execution, with Kubernetes-ready containerisation for production scaling. Stack: Node.js, Docker, Tailscale Funnel, MCP Streamable HTTP, AWS (S3, Lambda), Kubernetes.

  • AI Agent Development (Microsoft Copilot Studio): Designed, scoped, and shipped three internal AI agents — Enterprise System Agent, Email Manager Agent, and Employee Travel Agent — following a full engineering lifecycle (Requirements → MVP → Feedback Loop → MLP). Applied Requirements Engineering to scope agent capabilities with stakeholders across multiple internal departments. Attended industry seminars and conferences on the Microsoft Copilot and AI Agent ecosystem.

  • Billing Automation — P1 Production Incident: Mobilised on a P1 incident involving corrupted billing data threatening customer integrity. Built an automated remediation architecture using Power Automate and Snowflake SQL: intelligent SQL scanning across Assets, Contracts, and Subscriptions to detect failure patterns, context-aware triage logic, and closed-loop error logging back to Snowflake for full auditability. Gained real-world perspective on how architectural decisions impact business operations and customer trust.

items.position: Product Owner & Project Manager & Admin Secretary
items.description:
• Product ownership: Leading a team of UoA student developers building a custom full-stack web platform for AUSS — the largest sports club at the University of Auckland. Defined product vision, prioritised the backlog in Jira, and own end-to-end delivery from requirements to release.

  • Shipped features: User authentication with email verification, role-based access control (User / Admin), event management with RSVPs and capacity controls, executive team profiles, FAQ system, sponsor management with display ordering, and a media library.

  • Stack: React + Vite (frontend), Node.js + Express (backend), Prisma ORM + PostgreSQL, Docker for local dev environments.

  • Delivery: Run sprint planning, code reviews, and stakeholder communication with the AUSS executive committee; iterate features based on real club operational needs.

As a Teaching Assistant for ENGGEN 299: Workshop Practice at the University of Auckland, I facilitated hands-on learning experiences for second-year Bachelor of Engineering (Honours) students. This compulsory course introduces students to workshop safety and the use of various fabrication tools, such as laser cutters, 3D printers, soldering equipment, and machine shop tools, enabling them to create physical objects and mechanisms from diverse materials.

• JavaScript Development: Dedicated 50+ hours to learning JavaScript, focusing on DOM manipulation and web application development. Built a simplified e-commerce web application using vanilla JavaScript, HTML, and CSS, applying OOP principles and UI/UX best practices.
    🔗 GitHub: JavaScript Amazon Project

  • Application Development: Designed and built a cross-platform watermark removal tool for PDFs, images, and Word documents, addressing an internal organisational need. Integrated PyQt5, PyMuPDF, and python-docx for batch processing, fast/deep removal modes, and customisable settings.
    🔗 GitHub: Watermark Removal Tool

  • Agile Development Training: Completed 85+ hours of Agile methodology training, including daily stand-ups and mentor-led lessons. Gained hands-on experience in strict Agile workflows, project timelines, team collaboration, and best practices for high-quality software delivery.

Supervised daily restaurant operations, trained and mentored junior staff, managed scheduling, and coordinated with the kitchen on orders. Handled customer inquiries and complaints professionally while maintaining high service standards.

Prepared and served beverages, coordinated front- and back-of-house communication, and built strong relationships with returning customers. Resolved guest issues quickly to maintain high satisfaction.
Delivered individualised piano lessons to students of varying ages and skill levels, building personalised lesson plans and providing constructive feedback to track progress.

items.name: EVolocity — Real-Time EV Telemetry Dashboard (Capstone)
items.description: Final-year capstone project (Team 22, 4 members) building a real-time energy monitoring system for EVolocity's electric race vehicle competition. ESP32 boards sample ADC at 100 Hz and stream telemetry at 10 Hz over USB serial or MQTT; a Node.js backend ingests, normalises, and persists readings to SQLite, then pushes live data to a React + TypeScript dashboard over Socket.io. Features include dual-board fleet admin, sustained power-breach alerts, per-device config push (team ID, max power), downsampled historical telemetry queries, and an embedded Aedes MQTT broker with Last-Will disconnect detection. Stack: React + Vite + Tailwind v4, Node.js + Express + Aedes + Socket.io + better-sqlite3, ESP32 firmware (ESP-IDF) migrated from HTTP to MQTT. Comprehensive Jest test suite covering unit, integration, SQL-injection, and input-validation security tests.

items.name: EzShift — Shift & Payroll Management Web App
items.description: Team project with third-year Software Engineering students at the University of Auckland. Features role-based access control — administrators assign shifts and process payroll, while staff view schedules, submit feedback, and access payroll details. Backed by MySQL with a UI/UX inspired by leading SaaS products. Delivered using Agile methodology with modular implementation across HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and JSON. Next steps: finalising the backend framework and a scalable database schema.

items.name: Personal Portfolio Website
items.description: Modern, fully responsive personal portfolio built with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. Features a dynamic project showcase, smooth scrolling, and a mobile-friendly hamburger navigation.

items.name: Watermark Removal Tool
items.description: A user-friendly desktop tool for removing watermarks from PDF and Word documents. Offers both fast and deep removal modes using a combination of HSV layer and RGB mask processing. Built with Python, PyQt5, PyMuPDF, and python-docx.

items.name: Guess Who's The Thief
items.description: Interactive, AI-driven mystery game where players uncover clues, interrogate suspects, and solve an art-theft case. Combines gameplay logic with OpenAI-powered dialogue and voice interaction for an immersive experience.

items.name: Risk Map Game
items.description:
• Used graph data structures to model the game map — countries as nodes, borders as edges.

  • Implemented DFS and BFS algorithms for shortest-path calculation and border-crossing tax computation.

items.name: Venue Hire System
items.description: A comprehensive venue booking system with add-on services (catering, music). Built around OOP principles — inheritance, polymorphism, and encapsulation.

items.name: Odd & Even Hand Game
items.description: A human player faces off against an AI opponent (HAL-9000) that learns and predicts the player's behaviour. Combines chance, psychology, and a lightweight learning model.

items.name: Ring Counter (ATmega328P)
items.description: • Programmed an ATmega328P microcontroller in C to implement a binary up-counter and ring counter.

• Provided technical support to students and faculty, ensuring efficient operation of computer systems and software.

  • Maintained and troubleshot computer labs and IT equipment, resolving hardware and software issues to keep classes running smoothly.

  • Ran training sessions for students on technology tools and software applications.

• Prepared and assembled holiday packages for families in need as part of the Christmas packing crew.

  • Collaborated with volunteers to organise and pack food, toys, and clothing efficiently.

items.organization: Half Moon Bay Beach Cleanup Crew
items.description:
• Participated in organised beach clean-up events to remove litter and debris, contributing to environmental conservation efforts.

  • Worked with a team of volunteers to cover designated areas and educated community members on maintaining clean beach ecosystems.
